# Bug: pnpm-lock.yaml generated by aube only contains host platform optional deps

## Summary

When aube generates a `pnpm-lock.yaml`, it only includes optional dependency entries for the
current host platform. pnpm includes entries for all platforms.

This means a lockfile generated on Linux lacks `@esbuild/darwin-arm64`, `@oxlint/binding-darwin-arm64`,
etc. When a macOS developer runs `aube install --frozen-lockfile`, those binaries are not installed
and tools fail with "Cannot find native binding."

aube's native `aube-lock.yaml` format does include all platforms, but `pnpm-lock.yaml` does not.

## Steps to reproduce

On macOS (or any platform):

```sh
# Generate lockfile
aube install

# Check which @esbuild platforms are in the lockfile
grep '@esbuild/' pnpm-lock.yaml
```

## Expected

Lockfile contains entries for all common platforms:

```
@esbuild/darwin-arm64@0.25.9
@esbuild/darwin-x64@0.25.9
@esbuild/linux-arm64@0.25.9
@esbuild/linux-x64@0.25.9
@esbuild/win32-arm64@0.25.9
@esbuild/win32-x64@0.25.9
...
```

This is what pnpm generates.

## Actual

Lockfile only contains the host platform (e.g. on macOS ARM):

```
@esbuild/darwin-arm64@0.25.9
```

## Impact

A lockfile generated on one platform breaks `--frozen-lockfile` on other platforms. In practice,
CI (Linux) can't use a lockfile generated on macOS, and vice versa.

## Notes

The beta.6 release notes say cross-platform lockfiles only apply to `aube-lock.yaml`.
But pnpm's `pnpm-lock.yaml` format has always included all platforms. If aube claims pnpm
compatibility, it should match this behavior.

## Workaround

Use pnpm to generate the lockfile, then use aube to install from it. aube correctly reads
and installs from a pnpm-generated cross-platform lockfile.

## Versions

- aube 1.0.0-beta.9
- pnpm 10.33.0 (generates cross-platform lockfiles)
